metadata.summary: Run user services without root: enable linger, set resource limits, load secrets via EnvironmentFile, manage with journalctl.

content.title: systemd User Services
content.summary: Run applications as systemd user services (without root) with proper restart policies, resource limits, environment variables, and boot persistence. Enable linger, use absolute paths in ExecStart, set MemoryMax/MemoryHigh/CPUQuota, and replace cron with timer units for scheduled tasks.

why.p: Running applications with nohup ./app & provides no restart on crash, no resource limits, no log aggregation, and no boot persistence. User services give all of these without root. journalctl --user -u name gives structured logs with timestamps. systemctl --user edit name creates drop-in overrides that survive unit file updates.

list.item: Deploying Python/Node/Go applications that must restart on crash and start at boot
Managing multiple per-user services (Celery, FastAPI, Telegram bot) independently
Applying MemoryMax/CPUQuota resource limits to a service that has been OOM-killed
Adding scheduled tasks with better logging than cron (timer units)
Replacing screen/nohup patterns with proper lifecycle management
Services that must bind ports below 1024 — use nginx reverse proxy instead
Containerized workloads managed by Docker Compose — compose restart policies replace unit files
One-shot scripts run on demand — use cron or explicit invocation
When sudo -u or su is used to run the service — systemctl --user requires an active user session or explicit XDG_RUNTIME_DIR

rule.statement: Place application unit files in ~/.config/systemd/user/ and control them with systemctl --user. Never run application code as root via system services.
rule.rationale: User services are scoped to one user account, need no sudo to manage, and are isolated from system services. They work with the deploy script (rsync + venv) pattern and produce logs in journalctl --user -u name.

example.title: User service location and control
example.code: Location: ~/.config/systemd/user/nero-core.service Control: systemctl --user start|stop|restart|status nero-core Logs: journalctl --user -u nero-core -f Enable: systemctl --user enable nero-core (+ loginctl enable-linger)

section.name: unit-file-structure

rule.statement: Every unit file has [Unit] (metadata + dependencies), [Service] (execution + limits), and [Install] (boot integration). ExecStart must use an absolute path.
rule.rationale: systemd resolves ExecStart before starting the service and rejects relative paths. A relative path silently fails with an uninformative error. Always use the full path from the venv: /srv/nero/nero-core/.venv/bin/celery.

example.title: Complete three-section unit file
example.code:
[Unit] Description=NERO Core (Celery Workers) After=network-online.target Wants=network-online.target StartLimitBurst=5 StartLimitIntervalSec=60
[Service] Type=simple WorkingDirectory=/srv/nero/nero-core/src EnvironmentFile=/home/nero/workspace/.env ExecStart=/srv/nero/nero-core/.venv/bin/celery -A nero_core.celery worker --loglevel=info Restart=on-failure RestartSec=5
MemoryMax=8G MemoryHigh=6G CPUQuota=400% LimitNOFILE=65536 OOMScoreAdjust=0
[Install] WantedBy=default.target

section.note: Put StartLimitBurst in [Unit], NOT in [Service]. In [Service] it is silently ignored on systemd >= 229.

rule.statement: Run loginctl enable-linger once per user account. Without it, user services stop when the last login session ends and do not start at boot.
rule.rationale: By default, the user's systemd manager exits when the last login session (SSH, TTY, sudo) ends. Linger keeps a permanent user session alive from boot to shutdown, regardless of login state.

example.title: Enabling linger
example.code:
# Enable (run as root, or the user themselves) loginctl enable-linger nero
# Verify loginctl show-user nero | grep Linger # Linger=yes
# After enabling, enable and start services normally systemctl --user daemon-reload systemctl --user enable nero-core nero-channel-web nero-channel-tg systemctl --user start nero-core nero-channel-web nero-channel-tg

rule.statement: Set MemoryMax, MemoryHigh, CPUQuota, and LimitNOFILE on every production service. Without limits, a runaway worker can starve all other services on the VPS.
rule.rationale: MemoryHigh triggers memory reclaim before the hard cap; the service is throttled but not killed. MemoryMax is the hard backstop. LimitNOFILE=65536 ensures the limit is applied even if PAM is misconfigured.

example.title: Resource limit configuration
example.code: [Service] MemoryMax=2G MemoryHigh=1500M MemorySwapMax=256M CPUQuota=200% LimitNOFILE=65536 TasksMax=256 OOMScoreAdjust=-300 OOMPolicy=stop

rule.statement: Use systemd timer units instead of cron for scheduled tasks. Timers log to journald, support Persistent=true (catch up after downtime), and can be managed with systemctl --user.
rule.rationale: cron sends output to email (usually lost) and silently misses runs after reboots. Persistent=true on a timer runs the missed execution once after boot. journalctl --user -u backup.service gives the full run history with timestamps.

example.title: Timer unit configuration
example.code:
[Unit] Description=Daily backup timer
[Timer] OnCalendar=daily Persistent=true RandomizedDelaySec=300
[Install] WantedBy=timers.target

rule.statement: When a service fails to start, check journalctl --user -u name -n 50 --no-pager first, then systemctl --user status name. Most startup failures are captured in the last 10 log lines.
rule.rationale: The status output truncates logs. journalctl shows the full stderr/stdout from ExecStart, including Python tracebacks and missing module errors.

example.title: Troubleshooting table
example.code: Problem Cause Fix Service stops on logout Linger not enabled loginctl enable-linger "Failed to connect to bus" No DBUS in session Export XDG_RUNTIME_DIR Permission denied port <1024 User can't bind low port Use nginx reverse proxy ExecStart fails silently Relative path Use absolute path in ExecStart Secrets visible in show Inline Environment= Use EnvironmentFile= instead Restart loop (failed) StartLimitBurst reached Fix root cause; reset-failed OOM killed MemoryMax too low Increase limit or tune app

template.purpose: FastAPI/uvicorn service with resource limits and security hardening
Celery gevent worker with MemoryMax and CPUQuota
Timer + oneshot service for scheduled tasks
